name: Continuity Offense
description: A continuity offense is a pattern of movement, passing, and screening that flows continuously. If the primary scoring option is denied, the offense seamlessly transitions into the next phase without resetting, repeating the pattern on either side of the court until an open shot is created. It relies heavily on high basketball IQ, constant motion, and stamina.

playtypes.name: Cut
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player cuts without a screen (including UCLA, flex, etc.).

playtypes.name: Off-screen
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player uses an off-ball screen.

playtypes.name: Spot-up
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player stands still or moves without using an off-ball screen.

playtypes.name: Hand-off
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player receives the ball in a hand-off.
